motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: 3°F at first light, steadying to 7°F by noon. Sky clear and high and very blue. No wind to speak of — a southward drift, too gentle to be called a wind, the first movement of air in four days. The lake sealed and white and still.

undercurrent.note: The channel has delivered everything it came to deliver. Thirteen items in the box; no new ones today. The hum shortened again — six minutes twelve seconds — and the rowboat's shadow has corrected itself, which is the clearest sign the arc is resolving: things that were wrong are becoming right, not violently, but by degrees, the way the town always does things. Wren moved the cups. Agnes placed a classified for a chair. The undercurrent is dropping toward accommodation; the strangeness is not ending, it is settling in, becoming part of the town's fabric rather than its wound. The box will go back to page three. The hum will be shorter tomorrow. The returned will keep gathering, but more quietly, more routinely — the way the lake hums when it has said everything and is simply breathing. Level drops meaningfully today; stage holds at 3 because Agnes's chair classified and Toby's corrected shadow are loose threads still being braided into the ordinary, not resolved out of existence.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: Something is using the Gazette's lost-and-found as a postal address — and the letters it sends back are addressed to people who have not yet arrived in Greywater Falls.
